title: D3 Dashboard REST API + services/ middle layer
description: Extract pure-function business logic from existing MCP tools into services/{get-rules,append-intent,update-registry}.ts. Refactor tools/* as thin adapters calling services. Add REST API endpoints: GET /api/rules, GET /api/ledger, GET /api/scan, GET /api/human-lock (4 read endpoints) + POST /api/human-lock/approve, POST /api/intent/annotate (2 write endpoints). Add unified error body contract via api/_error.ts. Mount all API routes on the createFabricHttpApp Express app from TASK-006.

files.change: Extract core logic from tools/get-rules.ts: readAgentsMeta + human_locked_nearby computation + stale detection; return RulesResult type matching existing MCP tool response shape
Extract core logic from tools/append-intent.ts; accepts LedgerEntry from @fabric/shared; writes with source:'ai' tag; returns success/error
Extract core logic from tools/update-registry.ts; pure function with no transport coupling
Replace inline business logic with call to services/get-rules.ts; tool handler becomes: const result = await getRules(projectRoot); return formatMcpResult(result)
Replace inline business logic with call to services/append-intent.ts
Replace inline business logic with call to services/update-registry.ts

Export sendError(res, status, code, message) helper; reuse FABRIC_META_MISSING / FABRIC_META_INVALID error codes from meta-reader.ts; all API endpoints use this for consistent {error:{code,message}} responses
Express router: GET /api/rules calls services/get-rules; returns AgentsMeta with human_locked_nearby; 404 if .fabric/ missing
Express router: GET /api/ledger?source=ai|human&since=<isoTimestamp>; reads .intent-ledger.jsonl; filters by source and since; returns LedgerEntry[] from @fabric/shared
Express router: GET /api/scan calls createScanReport(projectRoot) from cli/scan; returns ScanReport JSON

GET returns HumanLockEntry[] with drift status (compare stored hash vs current file hash); POST /approve accepts {file, start_line, end_line, new_hash} body; updates human-lock.json locked[].hash; writes source:'human' audit entry to intent-ledger.jsonl
POST body: {ledger_entry_id: string, annotation: string}; appends new LedgerEntry with source:'human', annotation field, links to original entry via parent reference; returns created entry

files.target: Mount API routes on Express app
files.change: Import routers from api/rules, api/ledger, api/scan, api/human-lock, api/intent; app.use('/api', rulesRouter, ledgerRouter, scanRouter, humanLockRouter, intentRouter)

implementation: Create services/ directory with 3 pure-function files extracting logic from existing tool handlers; each service function takes (projectRoot: string, ...params) and returns typed result or throws FabricError
Refactor tools/get-rules.ts, tools/append-intent.ts, tools/update-registry.ts as thin adapters (each ~10-15 lines calling the service function and formatting MCP response)
Create api/_error.ts with sendError helper and error code constants
Create 4 read endpoints (rules, ledger, scan, human-lock GET) and 2 write endpoints (human-lock/approve POST, intent/annotate POST)
Update http.ts to mount all API routers; add express.json() middleware for POST body parsing
Regression test: existing MCP tool behavior unchanged — run existing tests if any

reference.pattern: Service-function middle layer pattern (SSOT for business logic)
reference.examples: computeAgentsMeta() in cli/sync-meta.ts:L71-99 is an example pure function; hashLockedContent() in cli/human-lint.ts:L115 for drift detection

criteria: GET http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/rules returns {nodes:[...]} JSON matching AgentsMeta shape
GET http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/ledger?source=ai returns LedgerEntry[] with only source:'ai' entries
GET http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/human-lock returns HumanLockEntry[] with drift:true for modified @HUMAN sections
POST http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/human-lock/approve with valid body updates human-lock.json locked[].hash correctly
POST http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/intent/annotate appends new source:'human' entry to .intent-ledger.jsonl
Existing MCP tools (get-rules, append-intent, update-registry via stdio) behavior unchanged after refactor to thin adapters

rationale.chosen_approach: Services layer as pure functions enables sharing between MCP tools and HTTP API without duplication; thin adapter pattern for tools minimizes risk of breaking existing behavior
alternatives_considered: HTTP API calling MCP JSON-RPC internally (adds parsing overhead and blurs boundaries)
Duplicating logic in HTTP handlers (maintenance nightmare as tools evolve)
decision_factors: SSOT principle: business logic in one place
Ritually-minimal write model: only 2 write endpoints (approve + annotate)
rationale.tradeoffs: Introduces an intermediate services layer; small complexity increase offset by reduced duplication

manual_checks: curl http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/rules — verify JSON matches AgentsMeta shape
curl 'http://127.0.0.1:7373/api/ledger?source=ai' — verify all entries have source:'ai'
Manually edit @HUMAN section content then curl /api/human-lock — verify drift:true on that entry
POST /api/intent/annotate — verify .intent-ledger.jsonl has new source:'human' entry
success_metrics: All 7 REST endpoints return correct HTTP status codes (200 for success, 404 for missing fabric)
MCP tools via stdio continue to work (regression: no behavior change)

risks.description: Refactoring MCP tools to thin adapters may introduce subtle behavior regression
risks.mitigation: Extract services first (add new files), then refactor tool handlers to call services; run any existing tests between each step
risks.fallback: If a tool regression is found, revert the tool adapter change and keep the original logic in the tool while still exposing via service for HTTP
